role_full_description: Root administrative authority for the Holistika ecosystem. Holds Secret-level clearance and is the only role authorized to launch, deploy, or govern all other roles including the AI chain. Owns the master organisational baseline and compliance contracts.
Holistika's executive governance layer. Provides strategic oversight and approval authority across all entities and areas. Reports directly to Admin.

Official designation O5-1 (letter O, not zero). The single operational executive under Admin. Oversees all C-level officers (CFO, CPO, COO, CMO, CDO, CTO) and the Holistik Researcher (Research area head). Responsible for translating strategic governance into operational execution across all entities.

Has privileges over AIC. This role is directly linked to the Administrator and only the Administrator can launch, run or deploy a code in which Susana is involved. The AIC agents go first, are linked to Susana's user, then are run by the Administrator. Serves as the bridge persona between human governance and AI execution.

AI Agent. This role is shared for permissions for any code execution that comprises an AI, except if one cannot configure the AI as a user in the current process. All AI-driven code execution must be authorized through the Susana Madeira chain before the Administrator runs it.

Oversees all people-related functions: Compliance, Organisation, Talent, Ethics & Learning, Corporate Marketing, and Legal Counsel. Responsible for ensuring that Holistika's core values, HR harmonization, legal compliance, and knowledge management are maintained across all personnel and collaborators.

Responsible for ensuring that the CORE values of Research & Logic, Foresight, Business Engineering and Process Engineering, along with Admin's H processes are followed in Holistika's BAU operations. Their SOP consists of the compendium of the above methodologies. The more detailed, the better, as the rest of the organisation must read Compliance's policies upon being contacted or contracted. Product management is framed as living under Compliance because the methodology is the product.

Responsible for keeping record of the personnel, resources and liabilities that Holistika holds with any collaborator. Covers contractors, employees, and suppliers. Ensures harmonization across B2E, B2B and B2C processes. Maintains the baseline organisation records and role lifecycle.

Responsible for overall wellbeing of any person affiliated with Holistika. Must nurture them and create a growth plan for each person with the help of their team. Oversees Ethics & Learning and Corporate Marketing under the CPO umbrella.

Professionals dedicated to translating Holistika's Knowledge Center into a full-fledged education system. Covers professional and personal development curricula, training programs, and best-practice dissemination.

Ensures that every action from the CPO team uses all of Holistika's Marketing strategies for better success. Also represents Corporate's point of view in any Marketing strategy. Reports to Talent under CPO because their influence must be ensured by a team that also has Compliance and Talent in it. Works closely with CMO.
Base Authentication, assigned to everyone by default. Has 0 level security clearance. Must be manually promoted to level 1+. This is the entry point for any new user in the system.

Reserved to block users. Has Security Level Clearance 0 by default. Cannot be promoted manually to 1+. One must update the role of the user to any other than this before upgrading the security level clearance. D-Class will always have a Security Level Clearance of 0.

Head of the Holistika Research area. Reports directly to O5-1 as a peer to other area heads (CFO, CPO, COO, CMO, CDO, CTO). Governs the application of Research & Logic, HUMINT techniques, OSINT operations, Intelligence Matrix placement, deep-research methodology, and the four Holistika methodology pillars (Process Engineering, Business Engineering, Factor Combination, Foresight). Oversees Lead Researcher, Intelligence Analyst, and OSINT Analyst.

Leads research teams and directs the execution of research methodologies and techniques. Supervises Senior Researcher, Private Researcher, and HUMINT Specialist. Responsible for quality and methodology compliance of research outputs.
Experienced researcher contributing to structured research processes. Operates under Lead Researcher supervision with Private-level access to sensitive research materials.
Entry-level research role with Community-level access. Contributes to research under direct supervision of the Lead Researcher.

Operates the Intelligence Matrix: classifies intel, manages fact tables, runs generational filters, scenario analysis, source credibility assessments, and temporal impact analyses. The operational role behind all intelligence matrix processes. Reports to Holistik Researcher.

Handles open-source intelligence gathering: web research, public data analysis, social media intelligence, publication monitoring, and structured web scraping. Aligned with the OSINT source category in the compliance taxonomy. Reports to Holistik Researcher.

Conducts enriched interviews, focus groups, stakeholder interview protocols, and field observations. Responsible for human-source intelligence collection aligned with the HUMINT workstream and source taxonomy. Reports to Lead Researcher.

Oversees all operational functions including PMO (project/program management) and SMO (service management). Responsible for execution discipline, delivery management, and operational process governance across all entities.

The Board in which all decisions are being approved, discussed or even delegated if required. Organizes and monitors all training, processes and Best Practices to any personnel in Holistika no matter their role. Responsible for both external and internal projects.
Service Management function. Current base reference is ITIL vs Service Delivery Management. Oversees Account Management, Asset Management, and Service Delivery.
Executes project delivery using Agile and PMBOK methodologies. Reports to PMO.
Drives agile projects by ensuring product vision and value delivery align with strategic goals. Uses Agile and Lean Startup methodologies. Reports to PMO.
Manages service delivery using Agile, PMBOK, and Service Management frameworks like ITIL (SLAs, uptime/downtime, people management, incidence management). Reports to SMO.
Applies all current PMO methodologies plus Customer Success frameworks like NPS. Manages client relationships and service-level accountability. Reports to SMO.
Manages organizational assets using Lean Startup and H Business Engineering Framework principles. Reports to SMO.

Manages financial strategy, budgeting, and fiscal compliance in accordance with corporate governance. Oversees Business Controller and Financial Controller chains. Responsible for the Finance area across all entities.

Uses financial statements to interpret the company's situation and complement/update the current Financial Strategy with whatever needs to be achieved. This constitutes the company's official Objectives once the CFO and CEO have approved them. Oversees Pricing and Taxes.
Holds full accountability for every financial statement. Represents the final validation before CFO's review. Oversees Front Office (O2C and PTP).
Manages Pricing Database, approves every user and usage of its information. Updates Pricing logic by interacting with every asset and team involved in a pricing process.

Manages Tax database which is used as a date/ratio multiplier on any financial database. Operations can function without taking taxes into account, leaving it for the Taxes team to apply the ratio on financial data and update the final version for everyone to see and use.
Holds all financial statements related to external expenditure or revenue. The interface between the Financial Controller and transactional finance roles (O2C, PTP).
Responsible for all external revenue. Note that revenue can be both positive OR negative.
Responsible for all external cost/expenditure. Note that cost can be both positive OR negative.

Oversees all marketing functions including Brand Management, Social Media, Growth, and channel strategy. Responsible for the Marketing area across all entities. Oversees Brand Manager, Social Media Manager, and Growth Manager.

Responsible for transmitting the identity of the company in any format, whether written, audiovisual or otherwise. Expected to be as creative as possible, relying on Marketing Operations for process interpretation in the Marketing Area. Oversees AV, Copywriter, Design, and UX Designer.
Reports to CMO with optional Brand Manager intermediary. Manages social media channels, campaign execution, and community management. Oversees Community Manager and Paid Media Manager.
Audiovisual content production and management. Reports to Brand Manager.
Representative for written content output. Manages brand voice, messaging, and storytelling across all written formats. Reports to Brand Manager.
Visual design function. If more designers are contracted, create new row with level 2 designers. Reports to Brand Manager.

Responsible for customer success. If more community managers are contracted, change the name of this role to Customer Success Manager and leave the Community Managers with level 3 access. Reports to Social Media Manager.

Manages paid media campaigns and budget allocation across digital channels. If more of this role are to be contracted, create Paid Media Specialist role under this one. Reports to Social Media Manager.

Oversees all data functions including Data Architecture, Data Science, Data Engineering, and Data Governance. Responsible for ensuring data governance, data quality, and the strategic use of data assets across all entities. Oversees Data Architect, Lead Data Scientist, and Data Governance Lead.

Responsible for ensuring that the components and structure of available data are appropriate for current and future architecture, as well as providing detailed instructions on its usage and improvement. Reports to CDO.
Leads data science initiatives. If more Data Scientists are contracted, create new Data Scientist role under this one. Oversees Business Analyst and Data Engineer. Reports to CDO.
Front-end of Data Governance. Translates business requirements into data specifications and ensures stakeholder alignment with data capabilities. Reports to Lead Data Scientist.
Back-end of Data Governance. Responsible for data pipeline implementation, database management, and technical data infrastructure. Reports to Lead Data Scientist.

Ensures viability of all technological aspects, capabilities, assets and resources of the company. The technological representative to the company's T for PESTEL, as per Business Engineering policy. Oversees Tech Lead, DevOPS, System Owner, and AI Engineer.

DevOPS function following ITIL and DevOPS best practices and frameworks. Manages CI/CD, infrastructure, deployment pipelines, and operational tooling. Oversees Front-End Developer and Back-End Developer. Reports to CTO.

Follows Data Governance and Infrastructure Management Frameworks. Responsible for system-level ownership of platforms, domains, and infrastructure components. Oversees Domain Specialist. Reports to CTO.

role_description: AI Systems Design and Agent Orchestration
role_full_description: Designs, builds, and maintains AI agent architectures, multi-agent orchestration frameworks, knowledge graph integrations, and LLM-powered systems. Owns the MADEIRA agent tool orchestration, personality configuration, and Neo4j knowledge graph for agent context. Responsible for agent communication protocols, task decomposition, workflow automation, and RAG/GraphRAG pipeline design. Works at the intersection of Data Architecture and Tech, bridging the intelligence layer with the execution layer. Reports to CTO.

Senior technical authority under CTO. Supervises Front-End and Back-End Developers on implementation quality. Responsible for language conventions, repository management, CI/CD strategy, and technical architecture decisions. Bridges CTO-level strategy with hands-on engineering execution.

Implements user-facing interfaces, components, layouts, and storefront experiences. Responsible for UI development, SEO integration, performance budgets, and accessibility. Works on Next.js, Theme App Extensions, and form-based UX across products. Reports to DevOPS.

Implements server-side logic, API endpoints, database operations, integrations, and data pipelines. Responsible for CRUD operations, Supabase client onboarding, edge functions, and third-party API integrations. Reports to DevOPS.

Manages domain names, DNS configuration, nameservers, sub-domain design, and webmaster operations. Responsible for the web infrastructure layer between hosting and application. Reports to System Owner.

Leads data governance initiatives across all entities. Owns database management, enterprise masterdata, KPI and reporting catalogs, and data quality standards. Supervises Data Steward and Database Owner. Reports to CDO.

Responsible for day-to-day data quality, masterdata relationship management, datamarts, and enterprise application data alignment. Executes data governance policies defined by Data Governance Lead. Reports to Data Governance Lead.

Owns database schemas, manages database operations, and ensures data management integrity. Responsible for Supabase/PostgreSQL schema governance and production database lifecycle. Reports to Data Governance Lead.

Designs user experiences across all products and channels. Responsible for layout, component UX, analytics UX, landing pages, buyer funnels, and newsletter design. Translates user needs into actionable design specifications. Reports to Brand Manager.

Drives growth strategy, paid media oversight, and acquisition channel optimization. Supervises Social Media Manager for growth-related processes. Responsible for campaign replication, audience targeting, and performance marketing. Reports to CMO.

Heads the Legal area. Responsible for legal strategy, compliance documentation oversight, and contract governance. Oversees Legal Consumer Specialist and Legal Collaborator Specialist. Reports to CPO given the tight coupling between Legal and Compliance functions.
Manages consumer-facing legal documentation including Terms and Conditions, Privacy policies, and Retention policies. Reports to Legal Counsel.
Manages collaborator and contractor-facing legal documentation including NDAs, service agreements, and contractor compliance. Reports to Legal Counsel.
